---
name: zlz77ghat-depth200-vacuity
description: ZopfliLZ77GetHistogramAt scores 0/41 kills — 8 is_fresh prologue pushes loop body past hardcoded --depth 200
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: f870ffcd-242f-412f-b0bb-2a8c1773beb7
---

`ZopfliLZ77GetHistogramAt` (Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code/zopfli.c) verifies SUCCESSFUL with a strong
memory-safety contract (is_fresh on lz77 + ll_counts/d_counts outputs + cumulative ll_counts/d_counts
+ ll_symbol/d_symbol/dists arrays, plus two foralls bounding symbol values < ZOPFLI_NUM_LL/_D),
but kill score is **0/41** — another [[avocado-depth200-vacuity]] case.

**Why:** all 41 mutants sit inside the 4 loops (copy + decrement, over ZOPFLI_NUM_LL=288 / ZOPFLI_NUM_D=32).
The contract needs ~8 is_fresh objects for the original's memory safety. That prologue makes even the
FIRST loop-body check (line ~1610, cumulative read) land at depth ~250-300, past the hardcoded `--depth 200`,
so every in-body check is vacuous. Verified: the `llpos = 288*(lpos/288)` → `lpos*288` mutant FAILS (real OOB)
at unlimited depth and at --depth 300, but SUCCEEDS (vacuous) at --depth 200.

**How to apply:** don't chase. Tried & ruled out: removing both foralls (threshold unchanged ~300) and
bounding `lpos < ZOPFLI_NUM_LL` to shrink cumulative arrays (unchanged) — cost is the is_fresh object count,
not array sizes or quantifiers, and can't be trimmed below threshold without dropping freshness the
original's decrement loops need. An ensures can't help either: body is cut before reaching it.
The counting semantics (ll_counts[j] = cumulative - #{i: ll_symbol[i]==j}) are also not expressible as a
CBMC ensures. Kill harness: Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code/kill_zlghat.py.

#!/bin/sh
# Mirror the verify CBMC pipeline (unwind 5, depth 200, enforce-contract).
# Usage: ./verify.sh <FUNCTION> [<FILE>]
set -e
FUNCTION="$1"
FILE="${2:-zopfli.c}"
goto-cc -o "${FUNCTION}.goto" "${FILE}" --function "${FUNCTION}"
goto-instrument --partial-loops --unwind 5 "${FUNCTION}.goto" "${FUNCTION}.goto"
goto-instrument --enforce-contract "${FUNCTION}" "${FUNCTION}.goto" "checking-${FUNCTION}-contracts.goto"
cbmc "checking-${FUNCTION}-contracts.goto" --function "${FUNCTION}" --depth 200
=====KILL HARNESS=====
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# Kill-test harness for ZopfliLZ77GetHistogramAt.
# Parses avocado mutants, applies each by line number to a fresh copy,
# runs the canonical CBMC pipeline (unwind 5, depth 200), reports kill/survive.
import subprocess, re, sys, os

SRC = "/app/Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code/zopfli.c"
FUNCTION = "ZopfliLZ77GetHistogramAt"
WORK = "/app/_kill_zlghat"

raw = subprocess.run(
    ["get-mutants", "--function", FUNCTION, "--file", SRC],
    capture_output=True, text=True).stdout

# Parse blocks
mutants = []
blocks = raw.split("--- original")
for b in blocks:
    m = re.search(r"@@ -(\d+) \+\d+ @@\n-(.*)\n\+(.*)", b)
    if m:
        ln = int(m.group(1)); orig = m.group(2); mut = m.group(3)
        mutants.append((ln, orig, mut))

print(f"Parsed {len(mutants)} mutants")

os.makedirs(WORK, exist_ok=True)
src_lines = open(SRC).read().split("\n")

killed = 0; survived = []
for idx, (ln, orig, mut) in enumerate(mutants):
    lines = list(src_lines)
    # line numbers are 1-based
    cur = lines[ln-1]
    if cur != orig:
        print(f"[{idx}] LINEMISMATCH ln{ln}: file={cur!r} expected={orig!r}")
        continue
    lines[ln-1] = mut
    mfile = os.path.join(WORK, "mutated.c")
    open(mfile, "w").write("\n".join(lines))
    g = os.path.join(WORK, "m.goto"); gc = os.path.join(WORK, "mc.goto")
    for f in (g, gc):
        if os.path.exists(f): os.remove(f)
    cc = subprocess.run(["goto-cc", "-o", g, mfile, "-I", "/app/Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code",
                         "--function", FUNCTION], capture_output=True, text=True)
    if cc.returncode != 0:
        print(f"[{idx}] CCFAIL ln{ln}: {mut.strip()}\n{cc.stderr[:300]}")
        continue
    subprocess.run(["goto-instrument", "--partial-loops", "--unwind", "5", g, g],
                   capture_output=True, text=True)
    subprocess.run(["goto-instrument", "--enforce-contract", FUNCTION, g, gc],
                   capture_output=True, text=True)
    cb = subprocess.run(["cbmc", gc, "--function", FUNCTION, "--depth", "200"],
                        capture_output=True, text=True)
    out = cb.stdout + cb.stderr
    if "VERIFICATION SUCCESSFUL" in out:
        survived.append((idx, ln, mut.strip()))
        print(f"[{idx}] SURVIVED ln{ln}: {mut.strip()}")
    elif "VERIFICATION FAILED" in out:
        killed += 1
        print(f"[{idx}] KILLED   ln{ln}: {mut.strip()}")
    else:
        print(f"[{idx}] ??? ln{ln}: {mut.strip()}\n{out[-300:]}")

print(f"\n=== KILLED {killed}/{len(mutants)} ===")
print("Survivors:")
for s in survived:
    print(f"  ln{s[1]}: {s[2]}")

import subprocess, sys, os
SRC="/app/Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code/zopfli.c"
base=open(SRC).read().split("\n")
# locate spec region: requires/assigns lines 1585..1601 (1-based) -> indices 1584..1600

# Identify markers
def build(spec_lines, mutate=True):
    lines=list(base)
    # spec region indices: find '__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(lz77' start
    start=None;end=None
    for i,l in enumerate(lines):
        if "__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(lz77, sizeof(*lz77)))" in l: start=i
        if "__CPROVER_assigns(__CPROVER_object_whole(d_counts))" in l: end=i
    assert start is not None and end is not None,(start,end)
    lines[start:end+1]=spec_lines
    if mutate:
        for j,l in enumerate(lines):
            if "size_t llpos = ZOPFLI_NUM_LL * (lpos / ZOPFLI_NUM_LL);" in l:

    if r.returncode!=0:

    subprocess.run(["goto-instrument","--partial-loops","--unwind","5","/tmp/v.goto","/tmp/v.goto"],capture_output=True)
    subprocess.run(["goto-instrument","--enforce-contract","ZopfliLZ77GetHistogramAt","/tmp/v.goto","/tmp/vc.goto"],capture_output=True)
    # binary search smallest depth that FAILS

        sstart=i; break

send=bopen-1  # last spec line index (inclusive) is bopen-1
# the mutated body line is after bopen
def build(spec_lines):

content.text:
## Summary

**`ZopfliLZ77GetHistogramAt` already verifies** — `VERIFICATION SUCCESSFUL`, 0 of 24816 properties failed — with its existing strong memory-safety contract (8 `is_fresh` objects + cumulative/per-symbol array freshness + two `forall` clauses bounding symbol values).

I tried to strengthen it by raising the kill score, but confirmed (matching the prior memory note) that **0/41 is inherent to the hardcoded `--depth 200` harness, not a spec weakness.** I built a per-line depth-attribution harness measuring the smallest `--depth` at which the earliest killable mutant (`llpos = 288*(lpos/288)` → `lpos*288`, an OOB read in the very first loop) actually fails:

| Spec variant | Kill threshold |
|---|---|
| Full valid spec | **295** |
| Remove both `forall`s | 350 (worse — they prune the path) |
| Lean valid spec (`lpos==size-1`, loops 2/4 empty, drop 3 big arrays) | 260 |
| **Absolute floor** (minimum to even reach loop1's OOB; invalid spec) | **215** |

Key findings:
- The prologue cost is the **field `is_fresh` allocations** (~20 steps each), and they're *gated* by `is_fresh(lz77)` — drop it and they all silently no-op (threshold collapses to 150), so they can't be cheapened without un-freshing `lz77`, which the decrement loops need for memory safety.
- The `forall`s actually **help** (removing them raises the threshold), so they stay.
- Even the **absolute theoretical floor of 215 exceeds 200**: no valid spec can reach the first OOB read within the depth budget. Every mutant sits inside the four loops, past the cutoff, hence vacuously "verified."

**Conclusion: don't chase.** The function is verified with a strong contract; the 0 kill score is a structural artifact of the depth-200 pipeline. The source file was not modified (all experiments ran on `/tmp` copies), and I updated the memory note with the sharpened, re-measured evidence.
